description: Adversarial invariant for CRISPR/gene-engineering semantic retypes: keep lineage edges separate from hard component dependencies.

checks.reason: Base editing descends from CRISPR-Cas9 editing but does not require double-strand-break CRISPR-Cas9 editing as the workflow component.
The hard base-editing component dependency remains on programmable Cas9 targeting.
Protein engineering enables programmable Cas9 use without being modeled as the only historical prerequisite for Cas9 nuclease discovery.
Prior genetic engineering is historical context for CRISPR-Cas9 editing, not the direct molecular component.
Recombinant DNA is the historical predecessor of later genetic-engineering platforms.
Genetic engineering enables synthetic biology while DNA synthesis and systems biology carry other parts of the scoped dependency.
The base-editing node should remain later than CRISPR-Cas9 genome editing.
Programmable Cas9 should remain anchored to the source-backed 2012 date.
PAM recognition constraints should stay compatible with the 2013 CRISPR-Cas9 editing node and the 2012 Jinek programmable-Cas9 source.
